# Smart City Lab (SML)

> Web app for managing members, task groups, tasks, points, files, calendar, and real-time whiteboards in a small lab/team. Stack: Node.js/Express + PostgreSQL + vanilla JS SPA + Python (WhisperX) ASR microservice. Built around a 12-column responsive masonry grid that admins can re-arrange live via an in-app visual editor, plus a `/dev` admin sandbox with experimental tools (room designer, audio recorder + Thai speech-to-text, point ledger, etc).

The codebase ships with a `docker compose` stack that boots the entire system (postgres, pgadmin, the Node app, and the WhisperX microservice). Source: https://github.com/NongKenKhung/SMLWeb. Schema migrations are forward-only and idempotent; the app auto-creates tables on startup.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): full project guide вЂ” architecture, quick start, env vars, /dev panels, deployment
- [asr-service/README.md](asr-service/README.md): WhisperX microservice configuration (model size, alignment, env vars)

## Browser routes

- [/](/ ): main SPA shell вЂ” login screen, then 6 tabs (Home, Todo, Calendar, People, Summary, Profile) on both desktop top nav and mobile bottom tabbar. The Whiteboard tab is hidden from both menus; the page is still rendered and reachable only by hash deep-link `/#whiteboard` or programmatic `setTab('whiteboard')`. Per-entity deep links: `/#tasks/<id>` and `/#calendar/<id>` auto-open the task / meeting detail sheet (via `_maybeAutoOpenFromHash` вЂ” waits 2 RAFs for `state.tasks` to populate); `/#summary/<groupId>` lands on group summary. The /dev whiteboard panel's "double-click card" handler uses these (`window.open('/#tasks/<id>', '_blank', 'noopener')`) so users can keep /dev open while editing.
- [/dev](/dev): admin-only sandbox SPA with 13 panels вЂ” entry point is the "рџ›  Dev & Test Tools" button in the Profile page (admin only). The active panel is persisted via URL hash (e.g. `/dev#whiteboard`) so refresh, bookmarks, and shared links all land on the same panel. `activatePanel(id)` is the single source for switching, called by sidebar clicks (which then `replaceState` the hash), by initial load (reads `location.hash`), and by a `hashchange` listener (covers back/forward + manual URL edits).
- [/healthz](/healthz): liveness probe (no auth)

## REST API

### Auth
- POST /api/login: { name, password } в†’ { token, user }
- POST /api/logout: invalidate session token
- GET /api/me: current authenticated user
- PUT /api/me/password: change PIN
- POST /api/me/avatar: multipart image upload

### Members + groups + people
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/members: member CRUD (admin only for write)
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/groups: task-group CRUD with leader + colour
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/connections: contact directory (kind + topics)
- POST / DELETE /api/groups/:gid/members/:mid: group membership

### Tasks
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/tasks: task CRUD; supports multi-assignee with role + points share. `DELETE /api/tasks/:id` is a soft delete (recycle bin); add `?permanent=1` to hard-delete.
- Meeting `location_type='onsite_internal'` auto-fills `location_detail='Lab @ECC-504'` as a sentinel. If the user later edits the meeting to `online` / `onsite_external`, the submit handler scrubs that sentinel out of `data.location_detail` so the rendered chip can't show "рџ’» Online В· Lab @ECC-504" (contradiction). A defensive helper `meetingDetailFor(t)` returns `''` whenever `location_type !== 'onsite_internal' && location_detail === 'Lab @ECC-504'` вЂ” used at every render site (`locationLabel`, `locationChipHtml`, calendar list, detail-sheet card, edit-form pre-fill) so legacy DB rows that were left stale before this patch still display cleanly without a data migration.
- Meetings (kind='meeting') accept both `deadline` (start) and an optional `end_time` (ISO datetime). End must be strictly after start вЂ” enforced at THREE layers: (1) the end-time flatpickr picker has `minDate` dynamically bound to the current start value so invalid times are physically greyed-out, (2) the meeting modal's submit handler throws "а№Ђаё§аёҐаёІаёЄаёґа№‰аё™аёЄаёёаё”аё•а№‰аё­аё‡аё­аёўаё№а№€аё«аёҐаё±аё‡а№Ђаё§аёҐаёІа№ЂаёЈаёґа№€аёЎ" if data slipped through, (3) `db.createTask` / `db.updateTask` repeat the check server-side. If omitted the ICS mailer defaults DTEND to start + 60 min. When the user changes start: end auto-shifts by the same delta to preserve duration; if the delta would make end <= start, end snaps to start + 60 min. Meetings never display "а№ЂаёҐаёў N аё§аё±аё™" overdue chips вЂ” past meetings just show their time range.
- GET /api/tasks/trash: list trashed tasks (admin sees all, group leader sees their group's). Must be registered before `:id` route to avoid Express path shadowing.
- POST /api/tasks/:id/restore: restore a trashed task
- UI exposes two ways to send a task to the recycle bin: the рџ—‘ button in the task-detail sheet, or by dragging a task card to the floating рџ—‘ bin on the Todo tab. Both fire `DELETE /api/tasks/:id` (soft delete). Neither asks for confirmation вЂ” restorable from рџ—‘ аё–аё±аё‡аё‚аёўаё° within 30 days. The one exception is meeting deletion via the detail-sheet button, which still confirms because the same call also sends CANCEL emails to attendees (not undoable). Setting `status='cancelled'` via the status field is a separate workflow вЂ” task stays visible but flagged as cancelled.
- All other destructive confirmations use `uiConfirm(message, opts)` (custom modal, async в†’ `Promise<boolean>`) instead of native `window.confirm`. Modal element is `#confirm-modal` in index.html. There's also a matching `uiPrompt(message, opts)` (modal `#prompt-modal`, async в†’ `Promise<string|null>`) used for free-text inputs like "add new category" so the native `window.prompt` is never called. Service worker `CACHE_VERSION` must be bumped whenever shipping a breaking change to `/app.js` so old precached copies get evicted on next page load.
- Date / datetime inputs in every modal form are wrapped by **flatpickr** (cdnjs 4.6.13, CSS allow-listed in `styleSrc`). Native HTML5 `<input type="date">` / `<input type="datetime-local">` follow the user's OS locale (mm/dd/yyyy + AM/PM on English Windows), which contradicts the project's "dd/mm/yyyy + 24-hour" convention everywhere else. flatpickr's `altInput: true` keeps the underlying input's value in ISO `Y-m-d` / `Y-m-d\TH:i` (so backend code is unchanged) while the visible field renders `d/m/Y` / `d/m/Y H:i` with `time_24hr: true`. Wiring point is `initFlatpickr(container)` in `app.js` вЂ” invoked from `openModal()` after `innerHTML` is set, and from the multi-task `addRow()` for dynamically-added rows. Filenames intentionally keep the `YYYYMMDD_` prefix style (separate format for FS sort-friendliness).
- Category (tag) picker in the task/meeting form (`_formCategoriesSection`): chips are **bucketed by `<аё«аёЎаё§аё”> - <аёЉаё·а№€аё­>` prefix** (Dev / Extrovert / аёЎа№‰аёІа№ЂаёЈа№‡аё§ / аёЁаёґаёҐаё›а№Њ / а№Ђаё­аёЃаёЄаёІаёЈ / аё­аё·а№€аё™ а№†) вЂ” each group has its own header card with emoji + count and chips inside. The chip body shows just the suffix (e.g. "Proposal") because the group header already provides the prefix context. Sticky **рџ”Ќ live-search input** filters by chip name OR group name and hides whole groups whose chips are all filtered out. **counter** "а№ЂаёҐаё·аё­аёЃ N / M" + **аёҐа№‰аёІаё‡аё—аё±а№‰аё‡аё«аёЎаё”** button. Within each group, **selected chips bubble to the top** via CSS `order: 1`. **+ а№Ђаёћаёґа№€аёЎ** opens `uiPrompt` (pre-populated with whatever the user typed in search), and the prompt hint recommends the `"аё«аёЎаё§аё” - аёЉаё·а№€аё­"` convention so the new chip auto-files into the right group. Helpers: `_splitCategoryName(name)` (parses prefix/sub), `_updateCatCounter()`, `_wireAddCategoryButton()` (binds prompt в†’ POST `/api/categories` в†’ re-renders section in place preserving selection). The picker is rendered in **2 places**: single-task edit form (`taskFormFields`) and the **multi-task batch form** (`openMultiTaskModal` вЂ” picker sits ABOVE the rows; selected ids apply to every task in the batch via one shared `category_ids` array). Intentionally **omitted from the meeting form** (`meetingFormFields`) вЂ” meetings already carry location_type + organisation context so the extra taxonomy clutters the modal without earning its keep. The form-wide submit collector still reports an empty `category_ids` array for meetings.
- File preview supports image / PDF / text / audio / video natively, plus Office. `.docx` uses **docx-preview** (loaded from **jsdelivr** вЂ” not published to cdnjs; depends on JSZip from cdnjs) which renders OOXML directly into a page-styled container so margins, fonts, colours, headers, inline images mirror the original. `.xlsx`/`.xls`/`.xlsm` uses SheetJS with a **custom table builder** (`renderExcelPreview` в†’ manual `<table>` generation rather than `XLSX.utils.sheet_to_html`) so column widths from `ws['!cols']`, merged cells from `ws['!merges']`, and number/date formatting from `cell.w` are preserved. Excel-style row + column headers, sticky on scroll. Numeric cells right-aligned via `class="xlsx-num"`. The xlsx panel uses `max-height: calc(95vh - 9rem)` + `overflow:auto` so both scrollbars sit next to the table (not at the modal's bottom edge). Libraries lazy-loaded on first preview вЂ” CSP `scriptSrc` whitelists both `https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com` and `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net`, and `frame-src` includes `blob:` so the PDF iframe can load blob URLs created via `URL.createObjectURL`. `.pptx` shows a download-only fallback (browser libs render too poorly to ship). Type detection uses extension regex (browser MIME for Office files is inconsistent). Helpers: `isWordFile`, `isExcelFile`, `isPresentationFile`, `isOfficeFile`; render funcs: `renderDocxPreview(blob)` + `_docxRenderInto(blob)` (2-step because docx-preview needs a mounted DOM target), `renderExcelPreview(blob)`.
- File-delete UX: the global `[data-delete-file-global]` click handler does optimistic DOM removal, then awaits `DELETE /api/files/:id`, then re-renders whichever sheet is open (submission or task detail) plus `loadAll()` for global state. The SSE-driven `debouncedReload` also re-renders the submission sheet when `state.openSubmitTaskId` is set, so file changes broadcast from another tab/user are reflected without manual refresh.
- POST /api/tasks/:id/claim and /drop: self-assign / unassign
- POST /api/tasks/:id/files: file or URL submission (multer). One `doc_type` per request via `?doc_type=` query string. The submission sheet stages each file with its own doc-type picker (bottom-sheet modal with full Thai labels вЂ” native `<select>` truncated long Thai text on mobile) and groups by doc_type client-side, firing one POST per unique doc_type. On-disk filenames use the pattern `YYYYMMDD_<docType>_<taskTitle>.<ext>` вЂ” the BODY is the TASK NAME (not the user's local filename) so the file browser stays self-documenting; the original filename is preserved in DB column `task_files.original_name`. The user's filename is only used to derive the extension. Multiple files of the same docType for one task get a `_xxxx` 4-hex collision suffix. When a task gathers в‰Ґ 2 attachments, they move into a subfolder `uploads/<groupName>/<taskTitle>/` вЂ” plain sanitized title, **no id suffix**. Two tasks with identical titles inside one group share the same folder; listing-by-task still works because the API queries `task_files` by `task_id` (not by reading the directory). Thai filenames round-trip correctly out of the box (multer 2.1.x + busboy 1.x decode `filename*=UTF-8''вЂ¦` per RFC 5987) вЂ” do NOT re-decode via `Buffer.from(name, 'latin1').toString('utf8')`, that destroys Thai code points. For legacy rows where an older browser sent the file via the old `filename="<raw bytes>"` form (which busboy decodes as Latin-1, producing strings like "Г В№вЂљГ ВёвЂћГ ВёВЈ..." in DB), `db.smartUtf8Decode()` is applied at READ time inside `listFilesForTask` / `listFilesForGroup` / `listAllFiles` / `getFile` / `generateGroupSummary`, and at WRITE time inside `recordFile`. The heuristic only re-decodes when every char is in 0x00вЂ“0xFF AND the byte sequence decodes as valid UTF-8 вЂ” so proper Thai/CJK strings (chars > 0xFF) and real Latin-1 names like "cafГ©" (invalid UTF-8) pass through unchanged.
- POST /api/tasks/:id/comments: thread comments per task
- GET / POST /api/deadline-requests: deadline extension flow
- GET / POST /api/point-requests: leader-review point pipeline (proposing в†’ leader_review в†’ final_review в†’ confirmed)

### Calendar + events
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/leaves: per-member leave records
- GET /api/stats: aggregate counts + scoreboard + upcoming
- GET /api/categories: connection topic list

### Whiteboards
- GET / POST / PUT / DELETE /api/whiteboards: collaborative drawing boards
- WebSocket /ws/whiteboard?board=ID&token=T: real-time per-board sync (Fabric.js canvas JSON)
- POST /api/whiteboards/:id/inject вЂ” body `{ kind, data }`. Generic вЂ” server only validates/broadcasts. Supported kinds (defined client-side in `WB_CARD_STYLE`): `task`, `meeting`, `group`, `recording`, `point_request`, `point_decision`. The рџ“Ґ button on the whiteboard toolbar opens a tabbed picker (`#wb-inject-modal`) with **4 tabs**: рџ“‹ Task / рџ“Ѓ Group / рџ“… Meeting / в­ђ Points (list pending `point_requests`, click вњ…/вќЊ to decide inline). Audio recording lives **outside the modal** вЂ” a dedicated рџЋ™ button in the whiteboard toolbar toggles record/stop, and a non-blocking floating widget (`#wb-float-rec` top-right of the canvas) shows the live timer + a вЏ№ button while recording. The recorder lifecycle is **decoupled from the modal** so users can close the inject picker and keep drawing/writing on the canvas while audio captures in the background. On stop в†’ uploads to `/api/recordings` в†’ auto-injects a `recording` card on the board. "+ аёЄаёЈа№‰аёІаё‡а№ѓаё«аёЎа№€" in the first three tabs reuses `openTaskModal` / `openGroupModal` / `openMeetingModal` with an `afterSave` callback that auto-injects the created entity. Card behaviour: **double-click** a card to (re-)open its full edit form (task / meeting / group), or to approve/reject for a `point_request`. `wbSyncCardsToState()` runs after every `loadAll()` so SSE-driven changes (Todo tab, another browser, /dev edit) update card text on the canvas without a manual refresh вЂ” uses cheap JSON diff per card identified by `_injectId`. The same 5-tab inject UI is ALSO available in the standalone `/dev в†’ рџЋЁ Whiteboard` panel (`dev.html` has its own copy of `WB_CARD_STYLE` + `_wbRowTask`/`_wbRowGroup`/`_wbRowPoints` + mini-recorder + `_wbCardDblClick` вЂ” adapted to dev's `apiFetch` wrapper). On dblclick of task/group/meeting cards, `dev.html` opens the main SPA in **EMBED MODE** inside an iframe (`#wb-edit-frame-modal`). URL is `/?embed=task|meeting|group&id=X` вЂ” main app detects `?embed=` early and adds `html.embed-mode` class which hides every shell piece (topbar, tabbar, login, page container) via CSS so only the edit form is visible. After load it calls `openTaskEdit` / `openMeetingModal` / `openGroupModal` automatically. The form's `closeModal` is patched to `postMessage({type:'sml-embed-closed'}, origin)` so dev auto-closes the iframe on save/cancel. iframe is same-origin so localStorage auth carries over. "в†— а№Ђаё›аёґаё”а№Ђаё•а№‡аёЎаё€аё­" promotes to a new tab; backdrop click + вњ• + Esc closes manually.

### UI / layout
- GET /api/ui-config: admin-defined layout (tab order + visibility, per-widget width/height)
- PUT /api/ui-config: save layout (admin only)
- POST /api/ui-config/reset: revert to defaults

### Audio recordings + transcription
- GET /api/recordings: list own recordings (admin: ?all=1 for everyone)
- POST /api/recordings: multipart audio upload вЂ” server stores blob + DB row, fires async WhisperX transcription. Accepts any audio extension ffmpeg can decode (mp3/wav/flac/m4a/aac/ogg/opus/webm/wma/aiff/amr/вЂ¦), up to 500 MB. The /dev в†’ рџЋ™пёЏ Audio Recorder panel exposes both рџ”ґ Record (browser MediaRecorder) and рџ“Ѓ Import (file picker for existing audio files)
- GET /api/recordings/:id/stream: stream audio with HTTP Range support (`?token=` query supported for `<audio>` tags)
- PATCH /api/recordings/:id: rename label
- DELETE /api/recordings/:id: delete row + on-disk file
- POST /api/recordings/:id/transcribe: manually retry transcription

### Point ledger (admin)
- GET /api/point-ledger: read-only audit of every point share вЂ” task title, group, role, phase, earned-at timestamp

### Real-time
- GET /api/events?token=T: Server-Sent Events stream вЂ” every successful mutating request fans out a `change` event so all open clients re-fetch

## Architecture

- **Backend**: Node 20 + Express + `pg` + `ws` + `multer`
- **Database**: PostgreSQL 16 (idempotent schema; no ORM)
- **Frontend**: Vanilla JS + Tailwind (CDN) + Fabric.js (whiteboard) + Three.js (3D room designer, lazy-loaded from cdnjs)
- **Speech-to-text**: PyTorch 2.5.1 (CUDA 12.1 + cuDNN 9) + Flask + WhisperX 3.8.5 (faster-whisper + Thai wav2vec2 alignment from `airesearch/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-th`). Default model: **`large-v3`** on **NVIDIA GPU** (`WHISPER_DEVICE=cuda`, `WHISPER_COMPUTE_TYPE=float16`, `WHISPER_BATCH_SIZE=16`). The `asr` compose service reserves a GPU via `deploy.resources.reservations.devices` (driver: nvidia, count: all). Image base is `pytorch/pytorch:2.5.1-cuda12.1-cudnn9-runtime` вЂ” ctranslate2 picks up bundled cuDNN/cuBLAS automatically. Audio is pre-processed through ffmpeg `dynaudnorm=f=200:g=15:p=0.9:m=10` before WhisperX so loud/quiet sections in phone-mic recordings get evened out (boosts quiet speakers up to +10 dB, keeps peaks under -1 dBFS). Toggle via `NORMALIZE_AUDIO` env (default `1`). The Node app does NOT call asr via the built-in `fetch()` вЂ” its underlying undici Agent imposes a 5-minute `headersTimeout` which fires before WhisperX finishes on slower paths. Instead it uses a `slowFetch()` helper built on `node:http` with a 15-minute socket timeout. Ollama calls follow the same pattern (10-minute timeout).
- **AI transcript organisation** (formerly summary): Ollama with `scb10x/llama3.2-typhoon2-3b-instruct` (Thai-tuned Llama 3.2 from SCB10X, ~2 GB). The prompt reorganises the transcript into a Markdown outline (`## аё«аё±аё§аё‚а№‰аё­` + `- bullets`) grouped by topic without paraphrasing speakers' actual points, plus a separate `action_items` array for explicit TODOs / decisions / assignments. Stored back in `recordings.summary` (Markdown string) + `recordings.action_items` (JSON array) вЂ” same schema as before. The /dev recordings panel renders the Markdown via an inline outline renderer (`## heading` в†’ `<h4>`, `- bullet` в†’ `<ul><li>`). Override via `OLLAMA_MODEL` env.
- **Real-time**: Server-Sent Events (`/api/events`) + WebSocket (`/ws/whiteboard`)
- **Layout**: 12-col CSS grid + ResizeObserver-driven masonry; mobile collapses to 1 col
- **Mobile / iPad / Apple Pencil**: every UI surface must work on phone + iPad + Apple Pencil. Concretely: inputs use `font-size: 16px` (gates iOS auto-zoom-on-focus) + `min-height: 44px` (Apple HIG); fabric canvases set `enableRetinaScaling: true` + `allowTouchScrolling: false`; their `.upperCanvasEl` filters `pointerType` so a `touch` event within 800 ms of a `pen` event is dropped (palm rejection); the wrap uses `touch-action: none` + `user-select: none` so native scroll/zoom can't hijack a stroke; toolbars switch to horizontal scroll under 640 px so no button can hide.
- **Deployment**: docker-compose with 4 services (postgres, pgadmin, app, asr)
- **NAS-ready storage**: docker-compose.yml ships with a dormant `sml_uploads` named volume (NFSv4, targeted at UGREEN NASync DH4300 Plus) вЂ” flip the commented bind-mount swap on `app` + `asr` to migrate `/app/uploads` off the host without code changes. `pg_data`, `asr_cache`, and `ollama_models` stay on local disk (latency / hot-cache concerns).

## /dev panels

The admin sandbox at `/dev` contains 13 panels organised into 3 sections:

### Main
- **API Playground**: hit any endpoint with custom method/body/headers + replay history
- **Data Explorer**: browse every table with row counts and quick filters
- **System Info**: env vars, SMTP info, DB stats, active sessions

### Lab (experimental)
- **Whiteboard**: real-time canvas (Fabric.js + WebSocket); sub-tab Audio Recorder; mobile/iPad/Apple-Pencil aware (palm-rejection via `pointerType` filter, `touch-action: none`, retina scaling, 44Г—44 toolbar buttons on phone)
- **Audio Recorder**: record в†’ upload в†’ server stores blob в†’ **WhisperX Thai transcription** with word-level timestamps; mobile-friendly with HTTPS hint, mic selector, live waveform, wake-lock
- **Component Lab**: live HTML/CSS/JS playground
- **Room Designer**: 2D + 3D floor-plan editor вЂ” 12-col snap grid, drag/resize/rotate, collision detection, layered items (laptop on desk), structures (column/wall) block layers, custom catalog, Three.js 3D view (left=select, right=orbit, middle/shift+left=pan), multi-room storage in localStorage
- **About Editor**: block-based CMS (h1/h2/h3, paragraph, image, list, quote, callout, link, code, video, columns) with preview mode + multi-page + JSON export/import
- **Point Ledger**: read-only audit of every point share with filter/search/sort/CSV export
- **Activity Log**: live SSE event stream tail
- **Dev Notes**: per-browser scratch pad

### Admin
- **Site Layout**: visual layout editor вЂ” drag widgets, resize columns + rows, toggle visibility per widget, for every page; "вњЏпёЏ Edit on Preview" mode embeds the real app in an iframe with on-widget handles, changes round-trip via postMessage
- **Settings**: system settings (admin toggles, SMTP enable, etc)
- **Members**: member CRUD UI

## Source files

The repo splits server code (`backend/`) from client assets (`frontend/`):

### Backend (`backend/`)
- [backend/server.js](backend/server.js): Express app, REST endpoints, SSE, WebSocket bridge
- [backend/db.js](backend/db.js): pg pool, schema init, CRUD helpers (single source of writes). `UPLOAD_DIR` defaults to `<repo>/uploads/` (`__dirname/../uploads`); override via env for NAS mounts.
- [backend/auth.js](backend/auth.js): file-backed token store with hot reload
- [backend/mailer.js](backend/mailer.js): SMTP for invitations and reminders. Calendar emails ship the ICS as a single inline `multipart/alternative > text/calendar; method=REQUEST` part (via nodemailer's `alternatives` option) вЂ” NOT through `icalEvent`, which would also add a duplicate `application/ics` file attachment and make iOS/Apple Mail render two copies of the same event. Modern clients (Apple, iOS, Gmail, Outlook 365, Thunderbird) handle the inline-only form correctly.
- [backend/seed.js](backend/seed.js): demo data seeder
- [backend/mock_files.js](backend/mock_files.js): sample-file generator for testing submissions
- [backend/test_api.js](backend/test_api.js): minimal smoke-test script for the REST API
- [backend/ecosystem.config.js](backend/ecosystem.config.js): PM2 config вЂ” alternative to Docker for bare-metal deploys

### Frontend (`frontend/public/`)
- [frontend/public/index.html](frontend/public/index.html): main SPA shell
- [frontend/public/app.js](frontend/public/app.js): main SPA logic (~6k lines)
- [frontend/public/style.css](frontend/public/style.css): Tailwind + iOS-flavoured custom CSS
- [frontend/public/dev.html](frontend/public/dev.html): /dev admin sandbox (~5k lines, single-page)
- [frontend/public/sw.js](frontend/public/sw.js): PWA service worker (stale-while-revalidate)
- [frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest](frontend/public/manifest.webmanifest): PWA manifest

### ASR microservice (`asr-service/`)
- [asr-service/app.py](asr-service/app.py): WhisperX Flask service (`/health`, `/transcribe`, `/summarise`)
- [asr-service/Dockerfile](asr-service/Dockerfile): Python 3.11 + ffmpeg + CPU-only torch
- [asr-service/requirements.txt](asr-service/requirements.txt): whisperx 3.8.5

### Root
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml): postgres + pgadmin + app + asr + ollama
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile): Node 20 image; `COPY backend ./backend` + `COPY frontend ./frontend` so container layout mirrors host. `CMD ["node", "backend/server.js"]`.
- [package.json](package.json): Node deps at repo root; scripts call `node backend/server.js` etc.
- [.gitignore](.gitignore): blocks .env, data.db, .tokens.json, uploads/*, .claude/, etc

## Configuration

Configure via `.env` at the project root. Required: `DATABASE_URL`. Optional: `ASR_URL` (set empty to disable transcription), `WHISPER_MODEL` (tiny/base/**small**/medium/large-v3), `WHISPER_LANG` (default `th`), `SMTP_*`, `TOKEN_FILE`, `TRUST_PROXY`. See [README.md#configuration](README.md) for the full table.

## Optional

- [pgadmin/servers.json](pgadmin/servers.json): pre-configured pgAdmin connection
- [mock_files.js](mock_files.js): sample-file generator for testing submissions
- [ecosystem.config.js](ecosystem.config.js): PM2 config вЂ” alternative to Docker for bare-metal deploys
- [test_api.js](test_api.js): minimal smoke-test script for the REST API
